latest.title: Monkeys sit on rocks but this is why they do it
latest.script_key: monkeys don t just chill on rocks they use them like lookout towers that higher spot gives them a clear view to spot predators or rivals before anyone spots them i always thought they sat randomly but that s why you ll see them pick the tallest rock in the enclosure

latest.title: Birds see ultraviolet patterns we miss
latest.script_key: birds see colors humans cannot see watch the feathers and eyes because many birds see ultraviolet detail hidden from us that extra color can help with mates food and navigation the same scene is richer to them than it is to us which bird fact next

latest.title: Cows remember faces across the herd
latest.script_key: cows can remember who belongs in the herd watch the calm face first because cows can recognize familiar herd members and people that memory lowers stress when the group feels predictable a quiet look can carry more history than it seems which farm animal should we explain next

latest.title: Lightning turns air into a shock wave
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next

latest.title: Dogs build a map with smell
latest.script_key: dogs build a map with smell watch the nose before the feet because every sniff samples layers of scent dogs can learn who passed where they moved and what changed since the last check the ground is basically a news feed what should they sniff next

latest.title: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Plants turn sunlight into stored sugar
latest.script_key: plants turn light into food watch the leaf surface because chlorophyll captures light energy and uses it to build sugar from air and water the green color is a tiny factory at work which plant clue should we decode next

latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Solar flares don't burn like you think
latest.script_key: that dark spot isn t a burn it s where the sun s magnetic ropes are twisting so tight they snap the flare s energy blasts away not down that s why the glow shoots outward painting auroras on earth did you picture a literal fire when you thought solar flare

latest.title: Lions hunt better when the pride coordinates
latest.script_key: lions turn hunting into a team problem watch the spacing before the chase because lions can use different positions in a group hunt one animal pressures while another blocks the escape path the power is not just muscle which predator tactic should we break down next

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: Plants use trap hairs to count touches before snapping shut
latest.script_key: plants use trap hairs to count touches before closing watch the trap hairs because the plant waits for more than one touch before spending energy on a snap that delay helps it ignore rain and debris then close when prey is real which plant trick should we decode next
